# TUI Layout & Display Requirements Quality Checklist: 005-tui-text-display

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of requirements for the TUI text display and input visibility fix — specifically the Static/dynamic layout split, flex protection, scrollback behavior, and markdown width.
**Created**: 2026-03-21
**Feature**: `.kilo/plans/005-tui-text-display.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the conditions under which InputBox/StatusBar become invisible explicitly documented as root causes? [Completeness, Plan §Root Cause Analysis]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the expected behavior of `<Static>` when items array receives new entries clearly specified (render-once semantics, immutability of rendered items)? [Completeness, Plan §Solution Design]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for what happens to completed tool calls when the assistant message is created — capture, clear, and lifecycle? [Completeness, Plan §Phase B3]
- [ ] CHK004 Is the full padding chain from terminal edge to markdown content documented with exact character counts? [Completeness, Research §Task 3]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements specified for the `CLEAR_COMPLETED_TOOL_CALLS` action — when it fires, what state it resets, and its interaction with `RESET`? [Completeness, Data Model §ChatState]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements for the streaming completion handler dependency array fully documented (which context values trigger the effect)? [Completeness, Plan §Phase B]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "terminal scrollback" defined clearly enough that a developer unfamiliar with Ink understands the distinction between scrollback and overflow-hidden clipping? [Clarity, Plan §Solution Design]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the phrase "always visible" for InputBox/StatusBar quantified — does it mean visible at all terminal heights, or only above a minimum? [Clarity, Plan §Key Behavioral Changes item 3]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the `columns - 8` width calculation justified with a breakdown that matches the actual component padding chain, not just an approximation? [Clarity, Research §Task 3]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the meaning of "rendered ONCE" for `<Static>` items precise — does it address what happens on terminal resize or React re-render? [Clarity, Plan §Architecture diagram]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "active content" vs "completed messages" boundary unambiguously defined — at what exact moment does a message transition? [Clarity, Plan §Key Behavioral Changes]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Are the padding values in the width calculation (`columns - 8`) consistent with the actual `paddingX` and `paddingLeft` values used in MessageBubble and the outer layout? [Consistency, Plan §Phase C vs MessageBubble.tsx]
- [ ] CHK013 Do the `MessageAreaProps` changes in the data model align with what the plan specifies for MessageArea simplification? [Consistency, Data Model §MessageAreaProps vs Plan §Phase B2]
- [ ] CHK014 Is the `overflow="hidden"` retained on the streaming-only MessageArea consistent with the claim that "content doesn't clip" after the fix? [Consistency, Plan §Solution Design vs MessageArea]
- [ ] CHK015 Are the `flexShrink={0}` requirements consistent between the plan (wrapper Boxes) and the research (Yoga behavior rationale)? [Consistency, Plan §Phase A vs Research §Task 2]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Are the performance goals ("completed messages cause zero re-renders") measurable and testable? [Measurability, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [ ] CHK017 Is the "streaming text renders at chunk speed without visible lag" goal quantified with specific latency or frame-rate thresholds? [Measurability, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK018 Can the requirement "InputBox and StatusBar always visible" be objectively verified in automated tests? [Measurability, Plan §Key Behavioral Changes item 3]
- [ ] CHK019 Are success criteria defined for the markdown width fix — what constitutes "no wrapping artifacts"? [Measurability, Plan §Phase C]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are requirements defined for the zero-messages state (fresh session, no conversation history)? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements specified for what happens when streaming ends but `completedToolCalls` is empty (text-only response with no tool calls)? [Coverage, Plan §Phase B3]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements for the dialog-open state clearly defined — does `<Static>` still render when `dialogIsOpen` is true? [Coverage, Plan §Phase B]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements for the `/clear` command interaction with `<Static>` defined — does clearing messages also clear scrollback? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK024 Are requirements specified for what `<Static>` renders when a user message has image attachments? [Coverage, Plan §Phase B vs MessageBubble images prop]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK025 Are requirements defined for extremely narrow terminals (e.g., < 40 columns) where `Math.max(40, columns - 8)` clamps the width? [Edge Case, Plan §Phase C]
- [ ] CHK026 Are requirements specified for terminals without native scrollback support (e.g., some CI environments, piped output)? [Edge Case, Research §Task 1 Alternatives]
- [ ] CHK027 Is behavior defined for very long conversations (hundreds of messages in `<Static>`) — any memory or performance concerns? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements specified for what happens when terminal resizes mid-stream (the `useTerminalSize` hook fires while streaming)? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK029 Is the behavior defined when `chat.completedToolCalls` reference changes between the effect check and the message creation? [Edge Case, Plan §Phase B3]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK030 Are accessibility requirements specified for the scrollback-based message history (screen reader behavior, keyboard navigation)? [Non-Functional, Gap]
- [ ] CHK031 Is the rendering performance improvement (O(n) → O(1) per chunk) documented as a measurable requirement, not just a design rationale? [Non-Functional, Research §Task 6]
- [ ] CHK032 Are non-interactive mode requirements (`--print`/`--file`) explicitly documented as unaffected by these changes? [Non-Functional, Plan §Constitution Check item 2]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK033 Is the assumption that "Ink's `<Static>` works as a sibling above `<Box height={rows}>`" validated with a specific Ink version requirement? [Assumption, Research §Task 1]
- [ ] CHK034 Is the assumption that `<Static>` items array preserves existing item references (for render-once semantics) documented as a requirement on the messages state management? [Assumption, Research §Task 5]
- [ ] CHK035 Is the dependency on `marked-terminal`'s internal code-block padding (4 chars) documented as a fragile assumption that may break on version upgrades? [Dependency, Research §Task 3]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items reference: Plan = `.kilo/plans/005-tui-text-display.md`, Research = `005-tui-text-display-research.md`, Data Model = `005-tui-text-display-data-model.md`
- Items are numbered sequentially (CHK001–CHK035) for easy reference
